moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Add quality tech, strong scanner setup

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Buy XLK at 10% to add a quality tech name with a strong scanner setup.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by reducing leverage and concentration.
A significant cash buffer will be held, and tight stops will be maintained.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leverage and diversifying, but potential upside is also limited. Expect lower drawdown volatility.

moves.note: Add quality tech, strong scanner setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name mandate and reduce concentration.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, reducing overall risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to balance income and broad market exposure.
Add to SMH, bringing it to 15%, and buy XLK at 10% to include quality tech with strong scanner setups.
This plan balances growth potential with a risk-first approach.
A moderate cash buffer will be maintained, and concentration will be reduced.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, aiming for risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index exposure with quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns and growth potential.

moves.note: Press what is working, strong setup
Press what is working, strong P&L

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name mandate and free capital for higher conviction plays.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reallocate capital into stronger, higher-scoring setups.
Increase SMH to 20% and CRWD to 15% to press into working positions with strong trends.
Buy XLK at 15% and META at 15% to concentrate in the highest-scoring scanner setups.
This plan stretches for growth by leveraging strong setups and allowing concentration.
A smaller cash buffer will be held, and wider but strict stops will be used.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. This approach significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this mode can lose a lot fast, requiring strict stop discipline.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leveraged and high-beta single-name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize income and monthly distributions.
Increase QQQ to 20% to provide broad index stability.
This plan focuses on parking cash for yield with a strong emphasis on income ETFs.
A cash buffer will be maintained, prioritizing low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on parking cash for yield and monthly distributions. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, cutting leverage and high-beta names. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
